title: RF Verification with Antenna Hats
summary: Conducted-over-the-air verification of spacecraft RF paths inside a payload processing facility using calibrated antenna hats and controlled EGSE.

steps.title: Confirm RF authorization
steps.description: Verify frequency coordination, facility approval, RF safety controls, transmitter authorization, command-security provisions, test roles, and stop-work criteria.

steps.title: Verify spacecraft state
steps.description: Confirm spacecraft configuration, grounding, power source, battery constraints, transmitter inhibits, safing plugs, software load, command path, and RF EGSE readiness.

steps.title: Verify antenna hats
steps.description: Confirm each hat identity, applicable antenna and band, polarization, serial number, calibration status, characterized coupling factor, connector condition, and cleanliness.

images.alt: Full-scale Europa Clipper high-gain antenna prototype undergoing range testing.
images.caption: Reference antenna test configuration for reviewing polarization, mechanical orientation, clearances, and interface identification.

images.alt: Technicians preparing the Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter high-gain antenna for a gimbal range-of-motion test in a payload servicing facility.
images.caption: Second reference configuration showing a spacecraft high-gain antenna and gimbal being prepared for a range-of-motion test, useful for checking antenna orientation, clearances, and interface access.

steps.title: Inspect antenna interfaces
steps.description: Inspect flight antenna surfaces, conductive gaskets, ground planes, nearby thermal blankets, cable interfaces, connector savers, and mechanical keep-out zones.

steps.title: Install antenna hats
steps.description: Install and secure each hat using the approved interface and torque or retention method. Verify alignment and clearance without loading, scratching, or contaminating the flight antenna.

steps.title: Connect RF EGSE
steps.description: Connect hats to the authorized RF rack using identified cables, calibrated attenuation, power limiting, directional devices, switching, DC blocks where required, and strain relief.

steps.title: Verify path loss
steps.description: With spacecraft transmitters inhibited, verify continuity, end-to-end insertion loss, switch state, isolation, termination, instrumentation protection, and safe injected-signal levels.

images.alt: Roman Space Telescope high-gain antenna in a blue RF anechoic chamber.
images.caption: Reference RF measurement environment showing absorber treatment used to suppress reflections during antenna characterization.

steps.title: Confirm RF containment
steps.description: Using the approved low-level source, verify the hat installation and facility controls provide acceptable leakage and do not create unintended coupling into adjacent spacecraft or facility systems.

steps.title: Verify uplink acquisition
steps.description: Apply the authorized low-level uplink and verify receiver acquisition, AGC or RSSI response, lock indication, command decode, command counters, and expected telemetry response.

steps.title: Characterize receiver path
steps.description: At approved test points, record frequency offset, modulation or data-rate behavior, link-quality telemetry, receiver switching, and any commanded threshold or redundancy checks.

steps.title: Verify downlink
steps.description: After authorization, enable the selected transmitter and measure coupled power, carrier frequency, modulation, spectral behavior, telemetry quality, and spacecraft housekeeping response.

steps.title: Exercise redundant paths
steps.description: Verify the prescribed combinations of redundant receivers, transmitters, transponders, antenna ports, RF switches, diplexers, and cross-strapped signal paths.

steps.title: Verify contingency behavior
steps.description: Execute approved command-loss, communication timeout, safe-mode, transmitter-inhibit, and controlled recovery cases while monitoring fault protection and configuration telemetry.

steps.title: Inhibit and disconnect
steps.description: Confirm all spacecraft transmitters are inhibited and injected RF sources are off before disconnecting EGSE, removing cables, releasing strain relief, or handling antenna hats.

steps.title: Restore flight configuration
steps.description: Remove hats, inspect flight antennas and adjacent surfaces, restore covers and flight configuration, reconcile hardware and calibration records, archive data, and document anomalies.
